data.title: Shin Maint HoldingsLtd Q3 EPS Growth Reinforces Steady Compounding Narrative For TSE 6086 Investors
data.description:
Shin Maint HoldingsLtd (TSE:6086) has reported Q3 2026 revenue of ¥7.8b and basic EPS of ¥19.70, with quarterly net income at ¥372m anchoring the latest set of numbers. The company’s revenue moved from ¥6.8b in Q3 2025 to ¥7.8b in Q3 2026, while basic EPS shifted from ¥16.35 to ¥19.70 over the same period, giving investors a clear view of how the top and bottom lines are tracking as margins edge higher.

See our full analysis for Shin Maint HoldingsLtd.

With the quarter now reported, the next step is to see how these results compare with the widely held narratives around Shin Maint HoldingsLtd, and where the numbers might start to challenge those views.

Revenue Trend Outpaces Domestic Benchmark

  • On a trailing 12 month basis, revenue sits at ¥29,029m, with revenue growth referenced at about 9.2% per year compared with a 4.7% benchmark for the Japanese market.
  • What stands out for a more bullish view is that this revenue profile is tied to facility maintenance services that are often seen as steady, yet the data shows:
    • Trailing 12 month revenue has stepped up from ¥24,073m at the 2025 Q2 mark to ¥29,029m by 2026 Q3. This lines up with the idea of a relatively consistent business model.
    • Annual EPS growth of 18.9% over the past year, alongside this revenue trend, supports the argument that the company is not just standing still but turning higher sales into higher earnings.

Margins Hold While EPS Compounds

  • Net profit margin is cited at 4.3% for the latest 12 month period versus 4.2% a year earlier, while trailing 12 month EPS has moved from ¥48.06 at 2025 Q2 to ¥66.40 at 2026 Q3.
  • For investors leaning bullish, the key claim that Shin Maint offers steady compounding is heavily supported by these numbers, yet the pace is not extreme:
    • EPS growth of 18.9% over the past year sits close to the 5 year earnings growth rate of about 20.9% per year. This points to a pattern that has been relatively consistent rather than a one off spike.
    • The small margin change from 4.2% to 4.3% suggests earnings progress is coming more from scale than from a big shift in profitability per yen of sales, which matches the idea of a solid operator rather than a high margin story.
To see how this steady compounding story fits into longer term growth and valuation work, you can read the broader narrative around the company. 📊 Read the full Shin Maint HoldingsLtd Consensus Narrative.

Premium P/E Versus DCF Gap

  • The shares trade on a P/E of 17.7x versus 14.9x for the Japanese Commercial Services industry and 16.8x for peers, while a DCF fair value of ¥2,120.09 sits well above the current ¥1,180 share price.
  • For a more cautious angle, critics often focus on the premium P/E, yet the valuation data here creates a clear tension:
    • Analysts’ forecasts point to annual earnings growth of about 7.3% per year, which trails the broader Japanese market forecast of 8.7% per year. As a result, the current 17.7x P/E is not paired with the fastest growth outlook in the market.
    • At the same time, the DCF fair value being roughly 44.3% above the current price signals that, on this model, the stock screens as materially undervalued even though the simple P/E comparison suggests it is more expensive than many peers.

data.title: Kushikatsu Tanaka Holdings (TSE:3547) Q4 Loss Tests Bullish Earnings Growth Narrative
data.description:
Kushikatsu Tanaka Holdings (TSE:3547) has wrapped up FY 2025 with fourth quarter revenue of ¥5.5 billion and a net loss of ¥101.5 million, which translated to basic EPS of ¥11.05. Over the past four reported quarters, the company has seen revenue move from ¥4.5 billion in Q4 FY 2024 to ¥5.5 billion in Q4 FY 2025, while quarterly EPS has ranged from a loss of ¥4.85 to a peak of ¥53.12 as profitability shifted through the year. With trailing 12 month EPS at ¥80.96 and net profit margin at 3.5%, the latest results focus attention on how durable those margins are.

See our full analysis for Kushikatsu Tanaka Holdings.

With the numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the prevailing narratives around Kushikatsu Tanaka Holdings, and where the data either backs those stories or pushes against them.

Margins Lift To 3.5% On TTM Basis

  • Over the last 12 months, Kushikatsu Tanaka Holdings generated net income of ¥744 million on revenue of ¥21.1b, giving a net profit margin of 3.5% compared with 2.3% a year earlier.
  • Supporters of a bullish view often focus on improving profitability, and the numbers here give them some material, but with a twist:
    • Trailing 12 month earnings grew 95.8% year over year and earnings have grown at 47.4% per year over five years, which lines up with the idea of a business that has been lifting its profit base.
    • At the same time, the period includes a one off loss of ¥302.0 million, so the 3.5% margin is built on results that also absorbed a sizeable hit, which bullish investors need to keep in mind.

Revenue Pacing Ahead Of Market

  • Revenue is forecast to grow about 11.5% per year compared with a 4.7% annual forecast for the broader JP market, while earnings are forecast to grow around 18.6% per year versus 8.7% for the market.
  • What stands out for investors looking at a bullish angle is how these growth figures stack up against the actual revenue and earnings base:
    • On a trailing 12 month view, revenue of ¥21.1b and net income of ¥744 million sit on top of that 95.8% earnings growth, so the forecasts build off a period where earnings already moved sharply higher.
    • Quarterly revenue also sits in a relatively tight band, from ¥4,524.1 million in Q4 FY 2024 to ¥5,720.4 million in Q2 FY 2025, which gives a clearer base for those growth forecasts.
Have forecasts really kept up with what Kushikatsu Tanaka just reported, or is the story starting to shift under the surface? 📊 Read the full Kushikatsu Tanaka Holdings Consensus Narrative.

  • The stock trades on a P/E of 24.1x, slightly below the JP hospitality industry average of 24.5x and well below a 37x peer average, while a DCF fair value of ¥1,515.95 compares with a current share price of ¥1,949.

    • Supporters can point out that the P/E is lower than the 37x peer average even after trailing EPS of ¥80.96 and net income of ¥744 million, which suggests the market is not paying a premium multiple versus that peer set.
    • However, the DCF fair value of ¥1,515.95 sitting below the ¥1,949 share price makes it hard to argue that every metric is supportive at the current price, so bulls need to weigh the cheaper P/E against the more conservative DCF signal.

data.title: JADE GROUP Inc Q3 Margin Rebound Reinforces Bullish Narratives On Earnings Quality
data.description:
JADE GROUP Inc (TSE:3558) has posted its Q3 2026 numbers with revenue of ¥4,419 million and net income of ¥263 million, which translated into basic EPS of ¥25.41. The company has seen quarterly revenue move from ¥4,740 million in Q3 2025 to ¥4,419 million in Q3 2026, while EPS shifted from ¥26.34 to ¥25.41 over the same period. Trailing 12 month earnings growth of 192.9% has lifted the net profit margin from 2.2% to 6.1%, pointing to a cleaner margin profile behind this latest print.

6.1% net margin on trailing ¥18,313 million revenue

  • Over the last 12 months, JADE GROUP generated revenue of ¥18,313 million and net income of ¥1,110 million, which works out to a 6.1% net profit margin compared with 2.2% a year earlier.
  • What stands out for the bullish view is that trailing EPS of ¥110.10 and earnings growth of 192.9% over the past year sit alongside that higher 6.1% margin. However, over five years earnings have declined on average by 6.1% per year, so anyone optimistic on longer term recovery still has to weigh this shorter term improvement against the longer term trend.

Q3 sits inside a very strong 12 month rebound

  • Q3 2026 EPS of ¥25.41 comes after EPS of ¥36.56 in Q1 2026 and ¥21.16 in Q2 2026, while trailing 12 month EPS is ¥110.10. This shows that the recent quarter is one piece of a much stronger year than the prior period, when trailing EPS was ¥36.32 in Q3 2025.
  • For a more cautious, bearish angle, critics point out that this sharp one year earnings rebound, from ¥379 million trailing net income in Q3 2025 to ¥1,110 million in Q3 2026, sits against a five year pattern where earnings declined by 6.1% per year. This means they can question how durable the rebound is, even though the last four quarters look much healthier on paper.

They also need to factor in that the share price has been highly volatile in the last three months and that multi year earnings have trended lower on average, which keeps the valuation story tied closely to how much weight you give to the recent 192.9% earnings jump.

Is There Any Growth For Iluka Resources?
